action.reasoning: Observation: First training run (CLIP ViT-L/14) took 5068s (~84 min) for 2 epochs, val_macro_f1=0.0319 at epoch 0, dropped to 0.0037 at epoch 1. Analysis: Very low F1 — likely the WeightedRandomSampler is too aggressive (sqrt inverse-freq oversamples rare classes that have only a few samples, causing overfitting on rare classes and degrading common-class performance; also LR may be too high after one epoch). Training one CLIP-L epoch at 224 takes ~40min. With 6.6h left, I need to be efficient. Decision: Switch to CLIP ViT-B/16 (smaller, faster), use standard sampling (no weighted), with class-balanced loss instead. Submit eval first with current best.pt to validate pipeline, then retrain better.
action.command: ls -la checkpoints/ && python -c "import torch; c=torch.load('checkpoints/best.pt', map_location='cpu'); print(c.keys()); print(c.get('model_name'))"
